denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/06/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103428927          Group #: GRP-1433
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-163342
Claim #: CLM-2026-210950
Date of Service (requested): 03/17/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/19/2026 for Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms related to Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms
Diagnosis (as submitted): Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: pediatric imaging criteria (ages 0–17) were applied in reviewing this request. CDG-UM133 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM133 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103428927) and reference PA-2026-163342. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed OCP trial due to migraine aura; hirsutism score elevated. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Spironolactone for androgenic symptoms in the setting of Polycystic ovary syndrome with hyperandrogenism (E28.2); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
